questions.question: CRC errors on an interface most often indicate…
questions.explanation: CRC failures mean the frame checksum failed — physical or duplex problems.

questions.question: Switch port forced full-duplex; PC auto-negotiates half-duplex. Expected symptom?
choices: Slow performance with CRC and late collisions
questions.explanation: Duplex mismatch causes errors and poor throughput while link may stay up.

correct.explanation: Duplex mismatch causes errors and poor throughput while link may stay up.

incorrect.explanation: **No connectivity at all** does not answer the requirement in this stem. Duplex mismatch causes errors and poor throughput while link may stay up.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "No connectivity at all" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Double speed** does not answer the requirement in this stem. Duplex mismatch causes errors and poor throughput while link may stay up.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Double speed" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **STP blocking** is the wrong device role here. STP prevents Layer 2 loops — it does not provide inter-VLAN routing, MAC learning rules, or replace trunk configuration.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Choosing STP when the question asks for forwarding, routing, or VLAN data plane behavior

questions.question: On a modern full-duplex switch access port, sustained collisions suggest…
questions.explanation: Full-duplex switched ports should not collide — suspect mismatch or bad media.

correct.explanation: Full-duplex switched ports should not collide — suspect mismatch or bad media.

incorrect.explanation: **Normal operation** reflects a common trap: Collisions are normal on all Ethernet links. Full-duplex switched links should have near-zero collisions.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Collisions are normal on all Ethernet links

incorrect.explanation: **OSPF adjacency** does not answer the requirement in this stem. Full-duplex switched ports should not collide — suspect mismatch or bad media.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "OSPF adjacency" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **DHCP renewal** does not answer the requirement in this stem. Full-duplex switched ports should not collide — suspect mismatch or bad media.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "DHCP renewal" without matching the scenario constraint

questions.question: First step when CRC errors increment on one link?
questions.explanation: Physical layer and speed/duplex alignment are the first checks for CRCs.

correct.explanation: Physical layer and speed/duplex alignment are the first checks for CRCs.

incorrect.explanation: **Reload the router** is a plausible symptom fix but does not match the most likely root cause here: Physical layer and speed/duplex alignment are the first checks for CRCs.

questions.question: True or False: An interface can show up/up while still accumulating CRC errors.
questions.explanation: True — link status does not guarantee error-free transmission.

questions.question: CRC errors appear only on a cable running parallel to power lines. Likely cause?
questions.explanation: EMI corrupts electrical signals on UTP — reroute cable or use shielded/fiber media.
